ab: harem kana wari salrangan ku kani nani mukuwa kivangavanga.
en: I went out with my mother today.

ab: nu ’uninan na wari mu na rukup mu temangiya, na wari mu ’azi aretid zi ’azi vias, zi valiyan na vali.
ch: 白天的天空很藍，天氣不冷也不熱，風很涼爽。
en: The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

ab: kazu mi kana talunan i itras maekan mi za pang, na senan na kadaw tu kadaway niyam zazek, salaw ta sadeku.
ch: 我們在草地上吃著麵包，陽光灑在身上，非常溫暖。
en: We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

ab: alusu na kadaw na rukup mu zemuni’ lra.
en: In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

ab: ku kiumalray i nani, kemazu zi zangzarang na rukup.
ch: 我問媽媽，為甚麼天空是紅色的。
en: I asked my mother why the sky is red.

ab: marengay i nani mu, alra mu raredek lra a variw.
ch: 媽媽說，可能是颱風要來了。
en: Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

ab: a ’aremeng mu i rukup iniyan za runev, mupana lra na vitu’en.
ch: 晚上天上沒有雲，星星出來了。
en: There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

ab: a muraruma’ mi i dadalan mu, tremepa ku kana vitu’en marengay zaku anger,
ch: 在回家的路上，我跟星星許願，
en: On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

ab: ’aziya mupana na variw, zi maranger ku ziya mupatraran mukuwa kivangavanga.
ch: 希望颱風不要來，我還想再出去玩。
en: I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.
